<Meta title="Foundations/Typography" />

# Typography

**Jump to:** [Usage](#using-typography) | [Content Standard Examples](#content-standard-examples) | [Web Components](#using-typography-in-web-components)

Our base font is **Inter**, a variable font with [many OpenType features](https://rsms.me/inter/#features).
It is available by default in Figma, although you can also [download](https://rsms.me/inter/) the font files; it is free and open-source.

We use five standard weights: 300 (light), 400 (regular), 500 (medium), 600 (semibold), 700 (bold). "Regular" is the default for most elements.

## Using Typography In Your Application

Our typography styles use CSS classes, rather than web components. To set type, you apply the relevant CSS class(es) to your element.

You should not need to create custom style definitions for typography styles;
the library's CSS classes should provide all you need.

### Translating Figma to code

For instance, let's say you used Figma in Dev Mode and saw this:

```css
/* Heading/L/Semi Bold/Default */
font-family: Inter;
font-size: 48px;
font-style: normal;
font-weight: 600;
line-height: 52px; /* 108.333% */
```

You don't want to drop that CSS raw into your application! Instead, notice that the
text is a Heading size Large, with a weight of Semi Bold. So you'd want to add these
classes to your HTML:

```html
<someHTMLtag class="atp-heading-l atp-font-weight-semibold"></someHTMLtag>
```

### Using Typography In Web Components

---

Since web components exist in the shadow DOM, they do not inherit any of the
CSS classes from the global stylesheet, which is where the typographic
classes are defined. Thus, if you are creating a web component, you'll need to use and apply
the _design tokens_ that were used to construct those typographic classes.

For instance, this is the definition for a typographic class:

```css
.atp-font-weight-bold {
  font-weight: var(--atp-font-weight-bold);
}
```

If the design for your web component calls for "FontWeightBold", you will need
to add this CSS to the styling for your web component:

```css
.my-web-component {
  font-weight: var(--atp-font-weight-bold);
}
```
